source: Statistics Canada, Canada's population clock (real-time model)
_README: Calibration snapshot for the Ooo! Pop Clock Mini. When enabled is true with a numeric population and an ISO-8601 capturedAt, the widget re-bases its projection to this official reading, so it only extrapolates the short time since capturedAt and stays within a minimal margin of StatCan's clock. ratePerSecond (optional) pins the speed too: here it is StatCan's own current rate, derived from their 'change since midnight' (817) over the 57058 s since Eastern midnight at capture = 0.0143188/s (~452k/yr, ~1237/day). Leave ratePerSecond null to keep the widget's year-over-year rate instead. To refresh: run `npm run pop-clock:rebase -- <population> [changeSinceMidnight]` (see scripts/rebase-pop-clock.mjs) or let the scheduled workflow do it. Snapshots older than 21 days, malformed, or future-dated are ignored automatically and the widget falls back to its own calculation.
